items.title: Manchester City and Spurs face vital games; Premier League and WSL news – matchday live
items.summary: ⚽ News, discussion and buildup before the day’s action ⚽ Today’s games | Tables | Team news | Email us here In the WSL today, the two most successful women’s teams in England go head-to-head in a London derby as Chelsea host Arsenal at Stamford Bridge. A win today would see the Blues move within three points of league leaders Manchester City, while victory for Arsenal could prove huge in the race for Champions League qualification and potentially – though unlikely at this stage – the title race. West Ham, who host Sunderland later, have agreed to terminate Guido Rodriguez’s contract by mutual consent. The Argentina midfielder is heading to Valencia. West Ham are also pushing to complete a deal for the Fulham winger Adama Traore. Continue reading...

items.title: Dimarco isoleerde zichzelf een maand om op niveau te komen
items.summary: Federico Dimarco nam na een teleurstellend seizoen een drastisch besluit: de speler van Internazionale zonderde zich een maand compleet af van de buitenwereld om zo sterk mogelijk aan de aftrap van het nieuwe seizoen te verschijnen.

items.title: 'I can't risk further damage' - Osaka withdraws from Australian Open
items.summary: Two-time champion Naomi Osaka has withdrawn from the Australian Open ahead of her match with qualifier Maddison Inglis, citing an injury.

items.title: Ajax gaat voor Carlos Vicente als nieuwe buitenspeler
items.summary: Ajax maakt werk van de komst van Carlos Vicente. De club ziet in hem de opvolger voor de naar Osasuna vertrokken Raúl Moro.

items.title: Fury: Wardley a 'good option' if Joshua fight can't happen
items.summary: Tyson Fury says a fight with heavyweight world champion Fabio Wardley would be a "good option" for him later in 2026 as he plots his return to action.

items.title: PEC Zwolle doet het opvallend goed tegen clubs uit linkerrijtje
items.summary: PEC Zwolle beleeft een curieus seizoen. De ploeg van Henry van der Vegt doet het goed tegen ploegen uit het linkerrijtje en juist minder tegen clubs uit het rechterrijtje. Dataman Bart Frouws en Sjoerd Keizer bespreken het in Scorebordjournalistiek.

items.title: Raducanu superfan ejected from Australian Open by police during De Minaur match
items.summary: The superfan who Emma Raducanu invited to her matches at the Australian Open has been ejected from the tournament by police.

items.title: Het nieuwe leven van Benjamin van Leer
items.summary: Benjamin van Leer (33) verkoopt tegenwoordig huizen aan de Costa del Sol, na een voetballoopbaan die hem (kortstondig) langs PSV en Ajax voerde. Hoe vergaat het de oud-keeper en partner van Lieke Martens? VI zocht hem op in Spanje.

items.title: 'Huijsen is de enige centrale verdediger die de Ballon d'Or kan winnen'
items.summary: Dean Huijsen maakt niet de beste periode uit zijn carrière door. De twintigjarige Amsterdammer werd bekritiseerd vanwege zijn matige spel bij Real Madrid, maar bij Bournemouth-directeur Tiago Pinto bestaat geen enkele twijfel dat hij er weer bovenop komt.

items.title: England take third wicket vs Sri Lanka as Mendis run out LIVE!
Ireland's Walsh claims impressive win at first Zuffa Boxing event
items.summary: Ireland's Callum Walsh claimed an impressive points victory over Carlos Ocampo as Dana White's Zuffa Boxing held its first event in Las Vegas on Friday night.

items.title: Oliver Glasner calls Palace truce with Parish: ‘We stick together, we work hard’
items.summary: Manager keen to finish season strongly after accusing chair of abandoning him and the squad by selling Marc Guéhi Oliver Glasner was about 54 minutes into his latest press conference when he laid out the plan for how his truce with the Crystal Palace chair, Steve Parish, could work after they ate together this week. “Steve and I left our dinner, and really both with a big smile we said: ‘Hey, we achieved so much all together here in the last 22 months. We don’t want and we don’t accept that this ends like the last three, four, five weeks have been. We don’t accept it.’ So we stick together, we work hard all together to get an ending this season that it deserves. Continue reading...

items.title: The influencer World Cup: Fifa and the TikTok deal targeting an avalanche of posts
items.summary: Partnership with tech giant speaks to push to engage younger fans but also has wider strategic goals in mind In this World Cup year, Fifa has come out of the blocks quickly. In the past few weeks any number of initiatives have been announced or activated, from a data partnership with Opta to facilitate more betting, to the Fifa Pass for speeding up visa applications for the US this summer, to the unveiling of the official Lego World Cup trophy. Among the ever-expanding list is an intriguing deal with TikTok, a partnership that will give digital creators front-row seats at the 104-match tournament. In Fifa language its partnership with the short-form video platform will make “the most inclusive event in football history … even more accessible”. According to TikTok’s global head of content, James Stafford, it will bring fans “closer to the action in ways they can’t get anywhere else”. It plans to do so by granting an unspecified number of online personalities behind-the-scenes access, giving them archive and highlights footage to use in their content and, in return, requesting an avalanche of posts that will make the World Cup inescapable for TikTok users. Continue reading...

items.title: Pressure firmly on Celtic in Scottish title race finally worthy of the name
items.summary: Sunday’s table-topping clash with Hearts is a fixture that carries huge meaning for both clubs It is instructive that Thursday evening’s Europa League clash in Bologna could be regarded by Celtic as an inconvenience. Aberdeen hold the Scottish Cup. St Mirren claimed the League Cup in December. Celtic find themselves involved in a title race worthy of the name. In short, domestic dominance is no longer a guarantee. Much has been said – and screamed – about the flow of poor decision-making that at least has Celtic’s hitherto immovable position in Scotland under threat. There has also been wild exaggeration in respect of the current crop of Celtic and Rangers players being among the worst in living memory. Celtic finished fourth and adrift of Motherwell in successive seasons from 1993. Rangers rattled around unconvincingly in the lower divisions, including a failed attempt to win promotion from the second tier, after their financial meltdown of 2012. The relative weakness of others in Scotland’s top flight is a reasonable point for debate but Old Firm fans have encountered much, much worse than this. Continue reading...

items.title: Tottenham, Bayern and a stand made of chairs - Palhinha's story
items.summary: On-loan Tottenham midfielder Joao Palhinha chats to BBC Sport's Kelly Somers about his life and career.

items.title: ‘We are fighters, it’s in our DNA’: Greenland find pride in rare tilt at futsal glory
items.summary: Buffeted by political storms and excluded from continental federations, Greenland find their chances are limited but a tournament in Croatia is a priceless opportunity Greenland’s futsal players string out in a line before angling their bodies to the left, facing the flag on the far wall. Nobody averts his gaze as the strains of their national anthem fill the hall. The red-and-white-halved banner, with its reverse-coloured semi‑circles, hangs comfortably among those of this week’s rivals. Scotland on the right, Morocco to the left; further along, there are even representations of Uefa and Fifa. The moment always feels special. Their long-serving coach, Rene Olsen, has been imagining it for several days. His team also know these occasions, all too rare, are to be seized. “It gives me goosebumps,” Patrick Frederiksen, one of their stars, will say later. “It’s when you realise that it is time.” Continue reading...

items.title: Infantino: Resale will drive up WC ticket prices
items.summary: FIFA president Gianni Infantino said that many fans who have applied for tickets for the 2026 World Cup will be looking to sell them for profit on resale sites, thus driving up the cost of ticket prices.

items.title: Berman: Rodman deal shows new rule is working
items.summary: NWSL commissioner Jessica Berman cited Trinity Rodman's new deal as evidence that the league's new High Impact Player is working as intended: to retain and attract top players.

items.title: German official calls for World Cup boycott talks
items.summary: A German soccer federation executive committee member says it's time to consider a World Cup boycott because of the actions of U.S. President Donald Trump.

items.title: Sources: Arsenal exploring Álvarez summer move
items.summary: Arsenal are exploring the possibility of launching a summer move for Atletico Madrid forward Julián Álvarez, sources have told ESPN.
